rulings.Case Title: ALBERT RICE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 6, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof based on factually devoid discovery responses. Plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue of fact. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's evidence are sustained. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: WETFEET, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. THE EMPLOYMENT CHANNEL, INC., A NEW YORK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re Ntc And Motion For Costs Including Attys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 6, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT BRODERICK BYERS' Motion For Costs Including Attorney's Fees is denied. Byers has failed to comply with the safe harbor provisions of CCP 128.7 and Byers has not shown he was the prevailing party. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: ATR-KIM ENG FINANCIAL CORPORATION VS. CARLOS ARANETA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn To Stay Litigation Pending Resolution Of Issues Of Philippine Law In A Philippine Court
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 6, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANTS SANTIAGO ARANETA, JUAN ARANETA, FERNANDO ARANETA AND MONICA ARANETA'S Motion To Stay Litigation Pending Resolution Of Issues Of Philippine Law In A Philippine Court. The motion to transfer based on forum non conveniens is denied. The Court has found that defendants may be sued in this Court. The fraudulent transfer action is distinct from the claims at issue in the Phillipines. There has been no showing that specific 3rd party witnesses will be unable to attend the trial of this action in California. The motion to stay the trial must be brought in Department 206. Certainly if an order is issued in the Phillipines precluding defendants from traveling to California to defend this action, that fact is relevant to the request to stay the current trial date. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: MALIMO'S A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. MALIBU PIER PARTNERS LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 6, 2008, line 5. CROSS COMPLAINANTS ALEXANDER LEFF, MALIBU PIER PARTNERS LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to August 12, 2008 on the Court's own motion. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA SCHAEFER et al VS. WILBUR WOO INDIVIDUALLY AND AS THE TRUSTEE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Pltfs Motion Seeking Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 6, 2008, line 6. PLAINTIFFS PATRICIA SCHAEFER, JEFFREY CRANDALL'S Motion Seeking Leave To Amend is denied with respect to causes of action 12 to 16. Plaintiff's have not met the requisite showing of either when the facts were discovered that support such claims and the facts plaintiffs point to fail to support the causes of action alleged. Granted with respect to the 17th cause of action for abuse of process and the 18th cause of action for slander. Facts are alleged. Any challenge to the causes of action under CCP 425.16 must be raised by motion. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN DUIGNAN et al VS. JOSEPH BETZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 6, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT JOSEPH BETZ'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained as to the first cause of action with leave to amend for plaintiff to attach the written contract. Overruled as to the other causes of action. Paragraph 8 pleads additional facts that provides a basis for the claims. =(302/PJM/VC)
